components.role: Non-inverting amplifier — gain set by feedback network Rf/Rg
Feedback resistor — sets gain as Av = 1 + Rf/Rg
Output coupling capacitor — blocks DC bias from output
Input resistor — DC return path to bias point

dsp_model.description: Clean gain with very wide bandwidth; effectively a gain block with coupling-cap highpass

behavioral_description: A clean, precise gain stage with virtually no harmonic coloration within its linear range. The non-inverting topology preserves signal phase and provides very high input impedance. Gain is set purely by the ratio of two resistors (Av = 1 + Rf/Rg), making it highly predictable. The bandwidth is extremely wide — far beyond audio — so this stage adds no tonal color of its own. When driven to clipping (approaching the supply rails), it produces symmetric hard clipping that sounds harsh, so this block is typically used well within its linear range as a clean boost or as the gain element in a clipping stage where separate diodes shape the distortion character.
